prompt: A climate research team records daily high temperatures for multiple cities. The TemperatureGrid class stores these readings in a private 2D double array named grid, where rows correspond to days and columns correspond to cities. Temperature data is supplied as a flat one-dimensional array in row-major order: the first cities values are the readings for day 0, the next cities values are for day 1, and so on. The class also stores the number of days and the number of cities as private int instance variables.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the TemperatureGrid constructor that takes int days, int cities, and double[] readings as parameters. The constructor must store the parameters in the appropriate instance variables, allocate grid as a two-dimensional double array of size days-by-cities, and assign grid[r][c] = readings[r * cities + c] for every valid row r and column c.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method cityAverage that takes an int parameter cityIndex and returns, as a double, the average of all values stored in column cityIndex of grid across all rows.

rubric.criterion: Constructor allocates new double[days][cities]; row and column dimensions are not swapped and are not hardcoded

rubric.point_label: Nested loops traverse all cells without bounds errors
rubric.criterion: Outer loop runs r from 0 to days-1 inclusive; inner loop runs c from 0 to cities-1 inclusive with no off-by-one
Source array indexed as readings[r * cities + c] or an algebraically equivalent expression
Assignment targets grid[r][c]; indices are not transposed to grid[c][r]
Loop iterates r from 0 to days-1 and adds grid[r][cityIndex] to a running sum variable
Returns sum / days (double division) after completing the loop; does not return sum alone or use integer division

test_cases.description: 3-day 2-city grid; verify city 0 average
2-day 3-city grid; verify last column average
Single-day 4-city grid; boundary check with one row

prompt: A school uses a digital seating chart system for classroom management. The ClassroomSeating class stores student names in a private 2D String array named seats, where rows and columns correspond to physical seat positions. Unoccupied seats are represented by the empty string "". The class also stores the number of rows and columns as private int instance variables.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the ClassroomSeating constructor that takes int rows and int cols. The constructor must store these values in the appropriate instance variables, allocate seats as a two-dimensional String array of size rows-by-cols, and initialize every cell to the empty string "".

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method assignSeat that takes a String parameter studentName. The method scans seats in row-major order and assigns studentName to the first cell that equals "", then immediately returns true. If no empty cell exists, the method returns false without modifying seats.

rubric.criterion: Constructor allocates new String[rows][cols]; row and column dimensions are not swapped
Nested loops in constructor cover every cell: outer loop r from 0 to rows-1, inner loop c from 0 to cols-1, no off-by-one

rubric.point_label: Each cell initialized to empty string, not null
rubric.criterion: seats[r][c] is set to "" (the empty string literal) inside the constructor loops; null or any other value does not earn this point
Nested loops scan seats row by row; outer loop iterates over rows, inner loop iterates over columns

rubric.point_label: Correct cell-selection, assignment, and early return
rubric.criterion: Condition tests seats[r][c].equals(""); on a match, assigns studentName to that cell and immediately returns true before continuing the loop

rubric.point_label: Returns false when no empty seat exists
rubric.criterion: Method returns false after the nested loops complete without finding an empty cell; does not throw an exception or return true

test_cases.description: 2x2 grid: fill all four seats then attempt a fifth
1x1 grid: first assignment succeeds, second fails
1x3 grid: fill all three seats then attempt a fourth

prompt: A data-processing utility operates on 2D int arrays representing survey responses, where each row is one respondent's set of answers. You will write two static methods for the GridUtils class. The first is a private helper used by the second. Assume every row in any input array has the same length and the array contains at least one row.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the private static method rowsEqual that takes two int array parameters, row1 and row2. The method returns true if and only if the arrays have the same length and every pair of corresponding elements is equal. Otherwise it returns false.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the static method eliminateDuplicateRows that takes a 2D int array parameter grid and returns a new 2D int array containing only the first occurrence of each distinct row from grid, in the order those rows first appear. Two rows are considered equal if rowsEqual returns true for them. The parameter grid must not be modified.

rubric.criterion: Returns false when row1.length != row2.length before entering the element loop
Iterates all indices 0 to row1.length-1; returns false on the first mismatched element; returns true only after all elements are confirmed equal

rubric.point_label: Result array allocated with correct dimensions
rubric.criterion: Allocates new int[uniqueCount][grid[0].length] using the computed count of unique rows; dimensions are not hardcoded or swapped
For each row i, compares it against every earlier row j (where j < i) using rowsEqual; marks the row as a duplicate only if at least one earlier row matches

rubric.point_label: Non-duplicate rows copied element by element into result
rubric.criterion: Each unique row is copied via an inner loop assigning result[nextRow][c] = grid[i][c] for each column c; nextRow is incremented after each copied row

rubric.point_label: Parameter not modified and new array returned
rubric.criterion: No element of grid is ever assigned a new value; the method returns result, the newly constructed array

test_cases.description: 3-row grid with one duplicate; result contains 2 unique rows
3-row grid with no duplicates; all 3 rows preserved
3-row grid where every row is identical; result has exactly 1 row

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the constructor PixelGrid(int rows, int cols). The constructor should allocate the 2D array grid with the given number of rows and columns, then initialize every cell to the String "white".

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method countColor(String color). The method should return the number of cells in grid whose value equals color. For example, if a 3-by-3 grid has exactly two cells equal to "red", then countColor("red") returns 2.

parts.label: (c)
parts.prompt: Write the method replaceColor(String oldColor, String newColor). The method should change every cell in grid whose value equals oldColor to newColor. Cells whose value does not equal oldColor are left unchanged.

rubric.criterion: Constructor allocates grid as new String[rows][cols] with rows and cols in the correct order (not swapped); hardcoding dimensions or reversing row/col order does not earn this point
Constructor uses nested loops to assign "white" to every cell; all grid.length * grid[0].length cells are initialized so that no cell remains null after construction
countColor uses nested loops with outer bound grid.length and inner bound grid[0].length (or equivalent), visiting every cell exactly once without an off-by-one error
countColor uses .equals(color) to compare each cell (not ==), increments an accumulator for each matching cell, and returns the correct total count
replaceColor checks each cell using .equals(oldColor) inside correctly bounded nested loops; cells not matching oldColor are never modified
replaceColor assigns newColor to grid[r][c] for each cell that satisfies the condition; every qualifying cell is updated and no non-qualifying cell is overwritten

test_cases.description: basic — set two pixels to red, verify counts, then replace red with blue
no matching color — countColor returns 0 and replaceColor changes nothing
replace entire grid — all cells are the same color

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the static method rowSum(int[][] scores, int row). The method should return the sum of all values in the specified row of scores. For example, if scores[2] is {80, 70, 90}, then rowSum(scores, 2) returns 240.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the static method keepStrongRows(int[][] scores, int threshold). The method should return a new 2D int array containing only those rows of scores whose row sum (as returned by rowSum) is greater than or equal to threshold, preserving their relative order. The original scores array must not be modified.

rubric.criterion: rowSum loops over columns using scores[row].length (or scores[0].length) as the upper bound, without an off-by-one error; iterating over the wrong dimension or using scores.length for columns does not earn this point
rowSum correctly accumulates each element of the specified row into a running sum and returns that sum; returning a partial sum or the wrong variable does not earn this point
keepStrongRows makes a pass over all rows and counts exactly how many satisfy rowSum(scores, r) >= threshold before allocating the result array

keepStrongRows allocates the result array as new int[count][scores[0].length] using the computed count of qualifying rows and the correct column size; swapping dimensions or using scores.length does not earn this point

keepStrongRows copies each qualifying row element-by-element with an inner loop (result[idx][c] = scores[r][c]) and advances idx only after a full row is copied; skipping the inner loop or advancing idx unconditionally does not earn this point

rubric.point_label: Original not modified and correct return
rubric.criterion: No element of the original scores array is ever overwritten (all writes target result), and the method returns result containing exactly the qualifying rows in their original relative order

test_cases.description: basic — three rows, two qualify at threshold 200; verify original unmodified
all rows qualify — result has same row count as input
no rows qualify — result is empty; original unchanged

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the method tallestInRow(int row) that returns the maximum plant height in the given row. If the row contains only empty cells (all zeros), return 0.

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method countTallPlants(int threshold) that returns the total number of cells in the plot whose height is strictly greater than threshold.

rubric.criterion: Method has signature public int tallestInRow(int row) and uses a single loop over columns of plot[row] (or plot[row].length) without bounds errors.
Initializes max appropriately (0 or plot[row][0]) and updates it whenever a larger value is encountered.
Returns the computed maximum after the loop; returns 0 when the row is all zeros.
Uses nested for loops with outer over plot.length and inner over plot[r].length (not hardcoded dimensions).
Compares each cell to threshold using strict greater-than (plot[r][c] > threshold) and increments a counter on match.
Returns the total count after both loops complete.

test_cases.description: basic row max and count
empty row returns zero
threshold boundary

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the constructor Theater(int numRows, int numCols). It must allocate the 2D array seats with the given dimensions and initialize every cell to the empty string "" (not null).

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method seatPatron(String name). It must scan the seats in row-major order (row 0 left to right, then row 1, etc.) and place name in the first cell whose value is the empty string. As soon as a patron is seated, the method must return true and stop scanning. If no empty seat exists, the method returns false and the array is unchanged.

rubric.criterion: Constructor allocates seats = new String[numRows][numCols] using the parameters (not hardcoded values, no swapped indices).
Constructor uses nested loops to assign "" (not null) to every cell of seats.
Uses nested for loops with outer over seats.length and inner over seats[r].length to scan in row-major order.
Tests each cell with seats[r][c].equals("") (or equivalent) to detect the first empty seat.
On the first empty seat found, assigns name to that cell and immediately returns true so no further cells are overwritten.
After both loops finish without finding an empty seat, returns false and the array is unchanged.

test_cases.description: seat first three patrons
test_cases.expected_output:
true
true
true
Ana
Ben
Cleo

test_cases.description: theater full returns false
empty cells initialized correctly

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the SeatingArrangement constructor as described above.

rubric.criterion: seats is assigned new String[rows][cols] using the parameter values (not hardcoded, not swapped).

rubric.point_label: Nested for loops with correct bounds
rubric.criterion: Outer loop iterates rows from 0 to rows-1; inner loop iterates cols from 0 to cols-1.
A counter (e.g., idx) is initialized to 0 and incremented exactly once for each name placed.
Code uses idx < names.length (or equivalent) to decide whether the current cell receives a name or the empty string.
When idx < names.length, seats[r][c] is assigned names[idx] (correct subscript order; not seats[c][r]).

rubric.point_label: Unused cells initialized to empty string (not null)
rubric.criterion: When idx >= names.length, seats[r][c] is explicitly set to "" rather than left as null.

test_cases.description: partial fill leaves trailing empty seats
exact fit with no empty seats
empty names array — all cells must be the empty string, not null

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Implement GridUtils.filterRows(int[][] grid, int threshold) so that it returns a new 2D array containing only the rows whose sum strictly exceeds threshold, preserving order and without modifying the input array.

rubric.point_label: Result 2D array allocated with correct row count
rubric.criterion: A new int[][] is allocated whose first dimension equals the number of rows whose sum exceeds threshold (e.g., new int[count][] after a counting pass, or equivalent).

rubric.point_label: Nested traversal computes each row's sum
rubric.criterion: Nested for loops are used to iterate every cell, with inner bounds grid[r].length, and a per-row accumulator is correctly initialized inside the outer loop.
A row is selected only when its computed sum is strictly greater than threshold (uses >, not >=).
For each selected row, a new int[] of the same length is allocated and each element of grid[r] is copied into it (or result[idx] holds an independent copy, not the same reference as grid[r]).
No assignment is ever made into grid[r][c]; all writes are to the new local result array.

rubric.point_label: Return value correct and rows in original order
rubric.criterion: The method returns the populated result array, with selected rows appearing in the same relative order as in grid; index advances by exactly one per selection.

test_cases.description: mixed grid: only one row exceeds threshold
no row passes; original array must be untouched
all rows pass and order is preserved

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt:
Write the static method tallestPerRow described above. Complete the method below.

public static int[] tallestPerRow(int[][] plants)

rubric.point_label: Result array allocated with correct length
rubric.criterion: Allocates new int[plants.length] (or equivalent) sized to the number of rows.
Outer for loop runs r from 0 to plants.length - 1 inclusive.

rubric.point_label: Inner loop bounds correct per row
rubric.criterion: Inner loop uses plants[r].length (not plants[0].length or a hardcoded value), correctly handling jagged rows.
Max-tracking variable is initialized so the first element of the row is considered (e.g. set to plants[r][0] or to a value that compares correctly against every element).
Inside the inner loop, the max is reassigned to plants[r][c] when plants[r][c] is greater than the current max.

rubric.point_label: Stores per-row max and returns array
rubric.criterion: Assigns result[r] = max for each row and returns the result array.

test_cases.description: rectangular grid
jagged rows
single row with negatives

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt:
Write the static method invert described above. Complete the method below.

public static int[][] invert(int[][] pixels)

rubric.point_label: New 2D array allocated with correct dimensions
rubric.criterion: Allocates new int[pixels.length][pixels[0].length] (or equivalent) — does not swap rows and columns or hardcode sizes.
Outer for loop runs r from 0 to pixels.length - 1 inclusive.
Inner for loop runs c from 0 to pixels[0].length - 1 inclusive.
Computes 255 - pixels[r][c] for each cell.

rubric.point_label: Value written to new array, parameter not modified
rubric.criterion: Stores the inverted value in result[r][c]; the parameter pixels array is never assigned to.
Method returns the newly built result array after both loops complete.

test_cases.description: small 2x3 image
parameter unmodified
single pixel

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write the constructor Warehouse(int rows, int cols). The constructor must initialize the instance variable inventory so that it has the given number of rows and columns and every shelf is empty. (Recall that a freshly allocated reference-type 2D array has all cells equal to null.)

parts.label: (b)
parts.prompt: Write the method addItem(String item). The method must scan the shelves in row-major order (row 0 first, then row 1, and so on; within each row from column 0 to the last column) and store item in the first empty shelf it finds, then return true. If every shelf is already occupied, the method must not modify inventory and must return false.

rubric.point_label: Constructor allocates 2D array with correct dimensions
rubric.criterion: Inside the constructor, inventory is assigned new String[rows][cols] using the parameter values (not hardcoded, not swapped).

addItem uses nested for loops with the outer loop iterating over row indices from 0 to inventory.length - 1 and the inner loop iterating over column indices from 0 to inventory[0].length - 1 (or equivalent) with no out-of-bounds access.
The body of the inner loop tests inventory[r][c] == null to identify an empty shelf.
When an empty shelf is found, the code assigns inventory[r][c] = item using the correct row index, column index, and parameter.
After a successful assignment the method returns true (or otherwise breaks out of both loops) so that no further shelf is modified by the call.
If both loops complete without finding a null cell, the method returns false and inventory is left unchanged.

test_cases.description: basic: place three items in a 2x2 warehouse, verify row-major order and that the unused shelf stays null
edge: fill a 1x3 warehouse completely, then attempt one more item which must return false
boundary: a single addItem on a 3x2 warehouse must place into [0][0] only and leave [0][1] null
